title: quercus sessiliflora var. cedrorum
intro: Quercus sessiliflora var. cedrorum, also known as cedar oak or evergreen oak, is a variant of the Quercus sessiliflora species, a type of evergreen tree native to the Himalayas. The leaves and bark of this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Quercus sessiliflora var. cedrorum include treating various ailments such as fever, cold, cough, and digestive issues, as well as being used as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or infusion for medicinal use, and the leaves are often dried and used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine.
